title: Mark a library as a reusable-tool extraction candidate
description: Tag an existing Nx library as generic enough to extract into the shared @bespunky libraries, and write the extraction.json marker the host-side extract-tool reads later. PREREQUISITE: the generic code must already live in its own clean Nx library — scaffold it with `nx g @nx/js:lib` / `@nx/angular:library` and move the code in first; recognising and abstracting the tool is your architecture-first judgment, this generator only marks it.

lib.description: Name of the existing Nx library to mark as an extraction candidate.
lib.x-prompt: Which library is generic enough to extract?

summary.description: One line: what the tool is and why it's generic.
summary.x-prompt: One-line summary (what it is, why it's generic):

rationale.description: Why it's generic enough to extract into a shared library (optional, longer).

package.description: Proposed published package name, e.g. @bespunky/<name>. Defaults to <scope>/<lib name>.

scope.description: npm scope for the proposed package.

kind.description: Library kind. 'auto' detects from the project's build executor and dependencies.
